ads.author: Matthew Haghighi
ads.title: DOUBLE DRAGON Arcade Cabinet – 1987 TAITO – Iconic Beat 'Em Up – Project / CRT / Original Wiring
ads.description: DOUBLE DRAGON Arcade Cabinet – 1987 TAITO – Iconic Beat ’Em Up – Project / CRT / Original Wiring Up for sale is an original Double Dragon arcade cabinet, one of the most important and influential arcade games ever made. Released in 1987 by Taito, Double Dragon is widely credited with defining the side-scrolling beat ’em up genre. This is the game that paved the way for classics like Final Fight, Streets of Rage, and countless others. It was one of the first true co-op brawler experiences, where two players could fight side-by-side through a continuous scrolling world—something that completely changed arcade gaming at the time. If you’re into arcade history, this is a big one. --- CONDITION / DETAILS: This is a project cabinet, and it’s priced accordingly—but it has a lot of the hard stuff already handled. Original CRT monitor – good working display New joysticks installed New buttons installed New control panel overlay New marquee Brand new side art package (cost me ~$200 alone) --- WHAT IT NEEDS: - Game PCB (board) is missing - Cabinet sides need: - sanding + finishing - Bondo work started on damaged corners but needs to be completed - Top panel is rough (see photos) --- WHY THIS IS A GREAT BUY: - Real 1987 Double Dragon cabinet – not a repro - One of the most iconic arcade titles ever made - Already has key upgrades done (controls, artwork) - Includes original CRT, not an LCD swap - Has correct wiring for an easy restoration - Perfect for: - Dedicated Double Dragon restoration - OR a JAMMA / multi-game build --- THE VIBE: This thing has that gritty, authentic late-80s arcade feel—the dragons wrapping the monitor, classic control panel artwork, and that unmistakable cabinet shape. Once finished, this will be a killer piece. --- BOTTOM LINE: This is a solid foundation project with a lot of money already put into parts and artwork. Finish it your way and you’ll have a legit, historically important arcade machine. Priced to sell.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Capcom Bowling, Upright Arcade, Capcom 1988 (Project Cab)
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Capcom Bowling (Capcom, 1988) Price: $200 (or best offer) This is a Capcom Bowling upright arcade cabinet in a Centipede Cabinet. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs a lot of love. - Monitor does not currently power on — likely needs new caps. - Nothing comes on at the moment; best to start troubleshooting with the power supply and work your way from there. - Sold as-is. Highlights: - Classic Capcom sports arcade with bowling gameplay. - Upright cabinet design from the golden age of arcades. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: - Price is $200, or best offer - Serious buyers only. Price Breakdown: Capcom Bowling PCS ($40) Control Panel and Trackball ($60) Cabinet ($100) CRT (dunno if its dead so $0?) Wiring, coin door, misc ($40)

ads.author: Dirk Smith Johnson
ads.title: Rare Williams OXO Pinball Machine
ads.description: Very rare Williams OXO pinball machine. Previously featured at the Roanoke Pinball Museum in Virginia. This is a unique electro-mechanical game that plays tic-tac-toe! This game has been in fully working condition until recently when it started blowing a fuse during game restart. It's most likely a switch contact somewhere that's broken, but I've been too busy to diagnose it, and I need the room, so I'm willing to sell it for less than I paid to someone who wants a unique game, and is willing to tinker with it. This game is basically in original condition except for being upgraded with $200+ worth of LEDs to make it brighter and more reliable.
